free_text: Yield of deep spallation products of medium to heavy
mass targets bombarded with 480 MeV protons
Resolution 18 keV FWHM at about 6 MeV.
Up to six targets could be mounted on a
target ladder and positioned remotely in front of a
gas filled chamber 5 mm in diameter and 4 mm in
length which was part of the gas jet transport
system. The nuclear reaction products recoiling out
of the target material were thermalized by the
ethylene carrier gas flowing through the chamber,
pressure about 10 Torr. The radioactive products
attached to molecular clusters (6-8) present in the
gas and were subsequently transported with the gas
flow, through polyethylene tubing (4 mm inner
diameter), to a collection chamber located
approximately 20 m away. The production cross
sections for the alpha emitters were calculated from
the measured activities using values of the
experimental parameters. The main source of error in
the cross-section values is the uncertainty in the
efficiency of the gas jet system. Unfortunately it is
difficult to estimate this precisely, and the same
holds true for the effective target thickness. This
difficulty is reflected in an estimated error of a
The main source of error in the cross-section
values is the uncertainty in the efficiency
of the gas jet system. Unfortunately it is difficult
to estimate this precisely, and the same holds true
for the effective target thickness. This difficulty
is reflected in an estimated error of a factor two.
